facts: Constellium operates a leased facility in White, Georgia, under its Automotive Structures & Industry segment.
Constellium's business is organized into three primary segments: Aerospace & Transportation (A&T), Packaging & Automotive Rolled Products (P&ARP), and Automotive Structures & Industry (AS&I).
For the year ended December 31, 2024, A&T accounted for 25% of total revenue and 50% of segment adjusted EBITDA.
For the year ended December 31, 2024, P&ARP accounted for 57% of total revenue and 43% of segment adjusted EBITDA.
For the year ended December 31, 2024, AS&I accounted for 20% of total revenue and 13% of segment adjusted EBITDA.
Holdings and Corporate operations recorded a negative 6% contribution to segment adjusted EBITDA for the year ended December 31, 2024.
Constellium reported total revenue of $7,335 million for the year ended December 31, 2024.
Cost of sales excluding depreciation and amortization was $6,397 million for the year ended December 31, 2024.
Depreciation and amortization expense was $304 million for the year ended December 31, 2024.
Selling and administrative expenses were $313 million for the year ended December 31, 2024.
Research and development expenses were $49 million for the year ended December 31, 2024.
Net income was $60 million for the year ended December 31, 2024.
Income before tax was $135 million for the year ended December 31, 2024.
Total shipment volumes reached 1,438 kilotons for the year ended December 31, 2024.
The average all-in aluminum price in the U.S. was $2,851 per ton for the year ended December 31, 2024.
Constellium's Automotive Structures & Industry segment generated $74 million in segment adjusted EBITDA for the year ended December 31, 2024.
Constellium's Packaging & Automotive Rolled Products segment generated $242 million in segment adjusted EBITDA for the year ended December 31, 2024.
Constellium's Aerospace & Transportation segment generated $285 million in segment adjusted EBITDA for the year ended December 31, 2024.
Holdings and Corporate operations recorded a negative $33 million contribution to segment adjusted EBITDA for the year ended December 31, 2024.
Constellium's total segment adjusted EBITDA was $568 million for the year ended December 31, 2024.
